title: 1603 Program: Payments for Specified Energy Property in Lieu of Tax Credits
summary: The 1603 Program was a historical clean energy subsidy initiative created under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009, through which the Treasury made direct cash payments to applicants in lieu of investment tax credits for specified energy property including solar, wind, geothermal, and other renewable technologies. The program disbursed over $26 billion across 109,766 projects estimated to power 8.5 million homes, with award sizes ranging from $180 to over $500 million.
key_points: Section 1603 was created under the American Recovery and Reinvestment Tax Act of 2009 to incentivize domestic clean energy production
The program provided cash payments in lieu of investment tax credits for specified energy property (solar, wind, geothermal, biomass, fuel cells, hydropower, etc.)
Over $26 billion was disbursed to fund 109,766 clean energy projects
Projects are estimated to produce enough clean energy to power over 8.5 million homes
Award amounts ranged from $180 to over $500 million
Recipients must submit annual performance reports for 5 years to maintain award eligibility
why_it_matters: This source describes a historical economic stimulus program from the American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 and has no relevance to current market structure or macro catalysts. It is an informational archive page about a concluded subsidy program with no implications for current fiscal policy, energy markets, or monetary policy.
